title: Documentation Block inline toolbar and contextual block actions in the editor
description: Per `plano-de-interface.md`'s `# Componentes` section ('Documentation Block' and 'Inline Toolbar') and `referencia-2.png` (the '/' slash-menu style block-insert picker), the editor's block-insertion UX should feel like a writing tool, not a form builder. `EditorToolbar.tsx` currently exposes a persistent toolbar (from TASK-79/insertBlockWithCaret). This task adds hover-triggered contextual affordances per block: a '+' to insert a new block below, and a '⋮' for block-level actions (delete, move, duplicate — whichever are already supported by the Tiptap node commands), matching the plan's 'ao passar o mouse, mostrar: + Adicionar bloco, ⋮ Mais ações'.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering over any block in the editor reveals a lightweight inline control (not always visible) offering 'Adicionar bloco' — opens the existing block-type picker (reusing TASK-28/71/72's `insertBlockWithCaret` machinery) positioned at that block

Hovering also reveals a '⋮' control for per-block actions; at minimum 'Excluir bloco' is supported (using Tiptap's existing node deletion), and 'Duplicar bloco' / 'Mover' are included only if achievable with Tiptap's built-in commands without new backend work — otherwise the menu ships with just delete and the gap is noted

The persistent top toolbar from TASK-79 is not necessarily removed — reconcile with this task's inline controls (e.g. top toolbar keeps global formatting/insert, inline controls become the primary block-insert entry point) — decide and document which one is now primary insert path so it's not duplicated confusingly
The block-type picker shown on '+' matches the plan's clean list-style menu (categories like Paragraph/Big heading/Bullet list, similar to `referencia-2.png`), not a dense icon grid
No regression in existing block-specific toolbars (Callout variant switcher, DosDonts variant switcher, ComponentEmbed picker, table controls) — those remain block-specific and untouched by this task
Editor keyboard-driven insertion (if any exists today) keeps working; this task adds a mouse-hover affordance, it doesn't remove existing entry points without a replacement

steps.details: Read `EditorToolbar.tsx`, `insertBlockWithCaret`, and each custom NodeView (`Callout.tsx`, `DosDonts.tsx`, `DosDontsCover.tsx`, `ComponentEmbed.tsx`) to catalog exactly what insert/delete/duplicate capability already exists at the Tiptap command level before adding new UI.

Implement a small floating control (likely via a Tiptap NodeView wrapper or a decoration-based approach) shown on block hover with '+' and '⋮' actions, styled per TASK-82's tokens (subtle, not heavy chrome).

steps.description: Wire '+' to the existing block picker
steps.details: Reuse the insertion logic already backing the top toolbar's buttons so behavior (and caret positioning) is identical — no parallel insertion implementation.

At minimum delete; add duplicate/move only if straightforward with Tiptap's command chain. Test on every block type including the custom NodeViews to confirm no conflicts with their own internal controls (e.g. Callout's variant switcher).

Decide the final division of labor between the persistent toolbar and the new inline controls; update/trim the top toolbar if it's now redundant, or clarify its remaining purpose (e.g. undo/redo, global marks).

technicalNotes: This is the most technically involved task in Fase 10 — it touches Tiptap NodeView/decoration internals, not just Tailwind classes. Budget accordingly; consider shipping delete-only '⋮' first if duplicate/move prove non-trivial with the current schema (blocks are serialized per TASK-30/31, so duplication needs a valid fresh block, not just a DOM clone).
Reuse `insertBlockWithCaret` (TASK-72's generic helper) rather than writing a second insertion code path.

`referencia-2.png` shows the exact visual target for the block-type picker triggered by '/' or '+' — match its list style (icon + label rows, grouped by AI/text/heading/list) rather than inventing a new picker layout.
